name: Parse documents with Google Drive and Airtable end to end

parameters.text: =🛑 Tonight's backup came out EMPTY — investigate immediately.

parameters.content:
### Hourly Schedule

Starts the workflow on a timer — every 1 h. No input data; downstream nodes fetch what they need.

Next: Export All Rows (Airtable)

### Export All Rows (Airtable)

airtable — `search` operation against the connected account.

Next: Serialize to CSV

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Airtable PAT

### Serialize to CSV

Serializes items to a CSV binary file.

Next: Upload Backup to Google Drive

### Upload Backup to Google Drive

googleDrive — `upload` operation against the connected account.

Next: Backup Size Sane?

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Google OAuth2

nodes.name: Note: Upload Backup to Google Drive

parameters.content:
### Backup Size Sane?

Two-way gate on `={{ ($binary.data?.fileSize ?? 1) > 0 }}`. TRUE continues the happy path, FALSE takes the alternate branch.

Ports: ①→ Tag Pipeline Metadata · ②→ Alarm — Empty Backup

### Alarm — Empty Backup

slack — `action` operation against the connected account.

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Slack OAuth2

### Tag Pipeline Metadata

Shapes the item: writes `pipeline`, `version`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Stamp Processing Time

### Stamp Processing Time

Stamps `processedAt` with the current time for auditing.

# Parse documents with Google Drive and Airtable end to end

**Pattern:** File Processing · **Level:** Foundation · **Trigger:** Scheduled

## What it does
Parse documents with Google Drive and Airtable end to end. It runs automatically on a schedule.

## Why it exists
Manual ocr & extraction is slow and error-prone; this workflow makes the process repeatable, monitored and hands-off, with failures surfaced instead of silently lost.

## Main flow
Hourly Schedule → Export All Rows (Airtable) → Serialize to CSV → Upload Backup to Google Drive → Backup Size Sane? → Tag Pipeline Metadata → Stamp Processing Time

## Use it for
- Teams that want to file processing without manual effort
- Anyone automating ocr & extraction in legal
- Replacing a fragile manual process with a reliable workflow

## You get
- Scales without extra headcount
- Removes copy-paste errors
- Improves response time

## Before first run
Connect credentials: Airtable PAT; Google OAuth2; Slack OAuth2.
Follow the color key: 🟦 entry · 🟪 AI · 🟫 actions · ⬜ utilities · 🟩 outputs · 🟥 error rail.
Each node has its own doc card underneath — read those before editing.

## 🛠 Customize me

Quick wins: point the data nodes at your own accounts (see 🔑 lines on each card), adjust the trigger cadence, and tune thresholds/labels to your ocr & extraction process. Every node card explains its role — safe to extend at any marked branch.
